mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or held near the lower bound, equity discount rates stay compressed and liquidity conditions support higher S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPI inflation is not accelerating, because stable prices keep real rates from rising.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average during periods of declining FEDFUNDS, or if the sign flips negative when CPIAUCSL is stable or falling.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above a recent baseline signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income expectations, which reduces risk appetite and pushes S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment rises are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of instances, or if PAYEMS does not confirm labor softening.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates stronger final demand and revenue growth, which supports corporate earnings expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if joint positive moves in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the signal better than the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO after a prior slowdown signals improving production and inventory replenishment, which raises cyclical earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change turns positive after being negative
failure_condition: Reject if post-decline rebounds in INDPRO do not lead to positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the effect persists only when broader demand proxies are also strengthening.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST after a prior uptrend signals cooling housing demand and weaker multiplier effects on consumption and construction activity, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if large housing starts declines are followed by nonnegative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the sign vanishes outside housing downturns.

mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS rate combined with elevated CPIAUCSL creates a real-rate and valuation shock that compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above CPIAUCSL inflation trend by a visible tightening cycle
failure_condition: Reject if tightening episodes with high CPIAUCSL are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns, or if FEDFUNDS alone explains outcomes without the inflation interaction.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS rises while UNRATE falls, labor income and job security improve together, supporting consumption and earnings expectations. This combination should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month, especially after weak labor readings.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS/UNRATE improvement does not precede positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal disappears when only one labor series moves.

mechanism_narrative: In a weak-demand regime, a concurrent decline in GDPC1 and RSAFS identifies broad revenue and output contraction that should weigh on earnings revisions and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not predict negative 60-day SPX returns, or if the pattern is neutral outside recessionary demand contractions.
